name: Regulatory compliance as quality driver
description: In regulated sectors, compliance requirements were identified as the primary forcing function for quality investment — often driving higher quality standards than practitioners would otherwise adopt.
key_evidence: HIPAA compliance drove near-zero PII leakage requirements in healthcare participants (INT-02, INT-08, INT-14)
EU AI Act high-risk classification created compliance urgency and threshold review (INT-07, INT-13, INT-14)
Financial regulation audit evidence requirements exposed logging gaps (INT-01, INT-07, INT-13)
significance_for_qalis: Motivates domain-specific threshold overrides and TI-4 (audit trail completeness). Strong signal from financial, healthcare, and legal sectors.
